features.description: Audit `TranscriberApp/Scribe/DesignSystem.swift` (1012 lines, pre-existing) against `design-reference/colors_and_type.css`. Add any missing tokens (colors, type sizes, spacing, radii, shadow values, motion durations, indicator state colors). Verify Inter Variable is registered at app launch and is the default font for every SwiftUI view in the Scribe target. Add unit tests verifying token resolution: live-dot color ΔE<2 vs reference, accent ΔE<2 vs reference, font-name resolution returns Inter. Do NOT modify the public API of existing `DS.*` tokens unless absolutely necessary; ADD missing ones rather than rename.
preconditions: Inter Variable woff2/ttf bundled in TranscriberApp/Scribe/Fonts/
expectedBehavior: All tokens in design-reference/colors_and_type.css have a matching DS.* Swift token (color, font, spacing, radius, shadow, motion)
Inter Variable is the default font of every Text/Label in the Scribe target; no SwiftUI default font fallback observable
Live-dot color resolves to the sRGB equivalent of oklch(0.55 0.14 35) within ΔE<2
Accent color resolves to the sRGB equivalent of oklch(0.50 0.09 255) within ΔE<2
New unit tests added under Tests/TranscriberCoreTests/ (or a new Tests/ScribeAppTests/ if present) cover token resolution; total tests >253

features.description: Rebuild the popover chrome and shell. Replace the current empty-state-driven RecordingMenu with a four-tab structure: Idle / Recording / Transcript / Settings. The Settings tab is an action that opens the existing SettingsWindow (do NOT host settings in the popover). The popover header has the four-bar Scribe mark glyph + lowercase `scribe` on the left, and a conditional `LIVE` indicator on the right ONLY when recording is active. The popover is 380pt wide, 12pt corner radius, with a translucent dark vibrancy chrome (HUD-window or popover material). Hide the default NSPopover triangular caret if visible (or accept it if AppKit forces it; document the choice). Preserve `NSWindow.sharingType = .none`. Add a `TabSelection` enum and routing logic; auto-select Recording when capture is active, Idle on stop. The Idle/Recording/Transcript pane bodies are placeholders in this feature (will be filled by features 3-5); show a single `Loading…` line for now.
expectedBehavior: Popover renders at 380pt wide with 12pt corner radius
Chrome uses translucent dark vibrancy material approximating rgba(22,22,22,0.78) with backdrop blur
Header shows mark + 'scribe' left and conditional LIVE indicator right (only when recording)
Four-tab segmented control beneath header with labels 'Idle', 'Recording', 'Transcript', 'Settings' in this order
Settings tab tap opens (or fronts) the existing SettingsWindow without changing popover body content
Recording tab auto-selects when recording starts; Idle auto-selects when recording stops
Confidential UI: NSWindow.sharingType remains .none
Idle/Recording/Transcript pane bodies show a placeholder (e.g., 'Loading…') — they will be filled in features 3-5
No 'READY' chip duplicate from prior pass remains anywhere
swift test passes; xcodebuild build passes
verificationSteps: swift test --package-path /Users/szymonsypniewicz/Documents/code/scribe
xcodebuild ... build
Manual: open popover, click each tab, verify selection state
Manual: trigger a recording, verify Recording tab auto-selects and LIVE indicator appears
Manual: click Settings tab, verify SettingsWindow opens and popover stays open
Manual: attempt screen capture, verify popover excluded

features.description: Build the Idle pane content for the popover. When recents are empty AND no upcoming calendar event: show three breathing pulse dots (small, animated), the headline `Listening.` (display weight, sentence-case-with-period), and the sub-line `Auto-records Zoom, Meet, Teams, FaceTime.` (muted secondary). When a calendar event is scheduled within the start-prompt window: show an `Up next` eyebrow (caps mono) above a single-row card with event title (left, semibold) and relative-time label (right, e.g., `in 5 min`). Below either, when at least one recent exists: show a `Recent` eyebrow followed by a list of up to 5 most-recent saved sessions. Each row: 26pt rounded glyph (left, with meeting initial or generic mark), title (semibold, single-line ellipsized), meta line `<when> · <duration>` (caption color), arrow `→` on the right that slides 3px on hover. Click a row to switch the popover to the Transcript tab populated with that session. Bind to existing recents data source (locate via grep — likely in AppDelegate or TranscriberCore Storage).
preconditions: An existing recents data source is available for binding
expectedBehavior: Empty state renders breathing dots + 'Listening.' + 'Auto-records Zoom, Meet, Teams, FaceTime.'
Up-next card appears when a calendar event is upcoming
Recents list renders up to 5 rows with glyph, title, meta, arrow
Row hover slides arrow 3px (or shows arrow if hidden by default — design ambiguous; either is acceptable as long as hover affordance is present)
Clicking a row switches the popover to the Transcript tab populated with that session
Empty recents → Recent section is hidden entirely (not shown with placeholder)
Reduce Motion: dots stop animating
swift test passes; xcodebuild build passes
verificationSteps: swift test --package-path /Users/szymonsypniewicz/Documents/code/scribe
xcodebuild ... build
Manual: open popover with no recents and no calendar — verify empty state
Manual: stage a calendar event 5 min in the future — verify up-next card
Manual: complete a recording — verify Recent section appears with the new entry
Manual: hover a recents row — verify arrow affordance
Manual: click a recents row — verify popover switches to Transcript tab

features.description: Build the Recording pane — the hero of the visual rebuild. Top row: rust live indicator dot + meeting title (semibold, ellipsized) + `via <source>` subtitle in muted text on the LEFT, large `mm:ss` elapsed timer (~22pt tabular numerals) on the RIGHT. Center: 56-bar mirrored animated waveform spanning the full pane width. Bars are 2pt wide with 2pt gaps. Heights follow a natural-speech envelope (per `RecordingScreen.jsx`: belly in middle, varied grain, range ~0.18 to 1.0). All bars animate continuously with a shared 900ms ease-in-out keyframe and per-bar staggered delays computed from index (`(i * 60) % 1800 - 900`). Below waveform: outcome strip `Recording locally · saved when you stop` (exact copy, U+00B7 mid-dot) in muted text. Footer: two right-aligned buttons — `Pause` (ghost variant) and `Stop` (secondary variant). Pause maps to the same handler as Stop (v1 UI-only mapping). Add `// TODO: real pause (post-v1)` at the Pause action handler. Add a stable bullet `OQ-PAUSE-V2: Real pause/resume during recording (resume into same transcript.md)` under `## Open Questions` in `docs/spec/SPEC.md`. Source-row binding: meeting title from matched calendar event title, fallback to recording-source app name (Zoom/Meet/Teams/FaceTime/system); `via` subtitle from recording source app. Reduce Motion: waveform freezes at static envelope.
preconditions: AppDelegate exposes recordingSourceLabel and outcomeFolderName (already added in prior polish)
Calendar matched event accessible from AppDelegate
expectedBehavior: Source row shows live dot, title, 'via <source>' subtitle, and large elapsed timer
Title binds to calendar event title when matched, source app name otherwise
56-bar waveform spans pane width with correct bar width/gap and envelope shape
Waveform animates continuously while recording with staggered per-bar delays
Outcome strip text is exact: 'Recording locally · saved when you stop'
Pause button maps to Stop in v1 (// TODO marker present)
Stop button finalizes the recording
OQ-PAUSE-V2 added to docs/spec/SPEC.md Open Questions section
Reduce Motion freezes waveform animation
swift test passes; xcodebuild build passes
verificationSteps: swift test --package-path /Users/szymonsypniewicz/Documents/code/scribe
xcodebuild ... build
Manual: start a recording, verify source row populates and timer ticks
Manual: verify waveform animates continuously
Manual: click Pause, verify same effect as Stop (recording ends)
Manual: verify exact outcome strip copy and mid-dot
Manual: enable Reduce Motion, restart recording, verify static waveform
Manual: read docs/spec/SPEC.md, verify OQ-PAUSE-V2 entry

features.description: Build the Transcript pane. Header: session title (h3 semibold) + meta line `<relative-day> <HH:MM> · <minutes> min · <speaker-count> speakers` (caption color). Body: render the markdown content of the selected session's `transcript.md` (or the most recent if none selected, or the active session if recording). Lines render as speaker-prefixed: speaker label (left, semibold, accent-tinted) + spoken text (right, body color). Footer: three text-with-icon buttons on left (`Copy`, `Export`, `Reveal`) and one icon-only delete button on right. Copy puts markdown source on clipboard; Export presents an NSSavePanel defaulting to `<title>.md`; Reveal opens session folder in Finder with `transcript.md` selected; Delete prompts confirmation and removes the session entirely (audio + transcript) — match existing settings-window delete-all-audio safety wording for the alert. Empty state (no recents): `No transcripts yet.` (sentence case, period). Active-session view: title from active session, body from any partial transcript content if available, footer hidden. Use existing `TranscriptStatusReader` and `TranscriptFrontmatterReader` for metadata; render markdown content via direct file read of `transcript.md`.
preconditions: Sources/TranscriberCore/Storage readers (TranscriptStatusReader, TranscriptFrontmatterReader) accessible from app target
expectedBehavior: Header shows session title + correct meta line
Body renders speaker-prefixed lines from transcript.md
Copy puts markdown source on clipboard
Export shows save panel with .md default extension
Reveal opens Finder with transcript.md selected
Delete shows confirmation alert and (on confirm) removes session directory
Empty state shows 'No transcripts yet.'
Active-session view: footer hidden, body shows partial content or 'Transcribing after stop.'
swift test passes; xcodebuild build passes
verificationSteps: swift test --package-path /Users/szymonsypniewicz/Documents/code/scribe
xcodebuild ... build
Manual: complete a recording, click recents row → Transcript tab populated
Manual: Copy → paste in TextEdit → verify markdown source
Manual: Export → save panel with correct default name + extension
Manual: Reveal → Finder opens with transcript.md selected
Manual: Delete → confirmation alert → confirm → session gone from recents
Manual: With no recents, switch to Transcript tab → verify empty state copy
Manual: During an active recording, switch to Transcript tab → verify footer hidden

features.description: Replace the current menu-bar status item icon with the design-system four-bar template SVG. Vector source: `.missions/scribe-visual-rebuild/design-reference/assets/menubar-icon.svg` (idle) and `menubar-icon-recording.svg` (active). Render as template image so AppKit tints monochrome. Idle: bar heights match `mb-bars[data-state='idle']` (5, 8, 6, 4 from CSS). Recording: same bars + 2.5pt rust dot at top-right corner; bars subtly pulse (scale-Y 0.7→1.25, 900ms ease-in-out, staggered per bar by ~110ms). Reduce Motion disables the pulse. If the user has enabled an inline elapsed counter setting (existing or to-be-added optional setting), the menu bar shows tabular-numerals `mm:ss` adjacent to the icon during recording. Click toggles popover. Bundle the SVG assets into Assets.xcassets as image sets if needed for proper template rendering.
expectedBehavior: Idle icon uses four-bar template SVG with correct heights
Recording icon shows rust dot top-right + pulse animation
Reduce Motion disables pulse
Inline counter (if enabled) shows tabular mm:ss adjacent to icon
Click toggles popover

features.description: Restyle the existing EndCountdownWindow and SavedNotificationWindow to match the design system. End-countdown HUD: 320pt wide, 14pt corner radius, dark vibrancy chrome with strong shadow; eyebrow uppercase mono caps `Stopping in` (or current copy if SPEC-locked) above a 56pt tabular-numerals count with weight 300 and small `s` unit suffix; 3pt-tall progress bar below count with fill that contracts toward zero; two equal-weight footer buttons (primary `Stop now`, secondary `Keep recording` — or current SPEC-locked copy). Saved-notification toast: 340pt wide, 12pt corner radius, dark vibrancy chrome; 30pt rounded 7pt-radius icon tile on left; stacked title (`Saved.` semibold) + sub (session title) + path eyebrow (mono caption with relative session folder path) in center; relative-time `when` (top-right small caps); slides in from top-right; auto-dismisses after ~5s with fade-out, or click opens session in Finder. Both windows preserve `NSWindow.sharingType = .none`. Verify Reduce Motion disables slide-in (use opacity-only).
expectedBehavior: HUD renders at 320pt wide with eyebrow + 56pt count + 3pt progress bar + two actions
Toast renders at 340pt wide with icon tile + title + sub + path + when
Toast auto-dismisses after ~5s or on click (opens session in Finder)
Both windows excluded from screen capture (sharingType = .none)
Reduce Motion uses opacity-only entrance (no slide)
swift test passes; xcodebuild build passes
verificationSteps: swift test --package-path /Users/szymonsypniewicz/Documents/code/scribe
xcodebuild ... build
Manual: trigger countdown via existing dev hotkey or silence detection, verify HUD chrome + count + progress
Manual: complete a recording, verify toast slides in with correct layout
Manual: click toast, verify Finder opens with session selected

features.description: Rebuild the SettingsWindow with the sidebar layout per `.settings-win` spec (states.css). Window: 760×700pt, 12pt corner radius, translucent dark vibrancy chrome, standard traffic-light controls left, centered title `Settings`. Body grid: 180pt sidebar (left, slightly darker bg, 1px right hairline) + flexible content pane (right). Sidebar rows: 16pt icon + label, hover treatment (4% white bg), selected treatment (8% white bg + white icon). Sidebar enumerates the panes the existing settings system already exposes (audit current SettingsWindow.swift to enumerate; expected: General, Storage, Diagnostics — preserve all). Each pane uses grouped rows: `s-group` containers (1px border, 8pt radius, 2% white bg), optional 9.5pt uppercase eyebrow group label, rows separated by 1px hairlines (4% white). Row layout: `lab` 12.5pt + `help` 11pt caption on left, control on right. Toggles use the `osw` macOS-style switch (30×18pt, knob 14pt, off=10% white bg, on=oklch(0.55 0.18 145)). Inputs use `s-input` styling. Storage pane: `Save to` row with current path + `Choose…` ghost button + `Reveal in Finder` row. Storage panel from prior pass (audio bytes used, refresh, delete-all-audio with confirmation) preserved with restyling. Diagnostics pane: 4-cell `diag` strip (Sessions / Errors / Engine / Disk) at top + existing diagnostics export button. Window's `NSWindow.sharingType = .none`. Bind ALL controls to the existing SettingsStore — do NOT introduce a new store.
preconditions: Existing SettingsStore + SettingsWindow + DiagnosticsView available for restyling
expectedBehavior: Window renders at 760×700pt with sidebar + content layout
Sidebar enumerates all current settings panes with hover + selected treatments
All panes use grouped-row layout with 1px borders + hairlines
Toggles render as macOS-style switches with correct on/off colors
ElevenLabs API key field shows masked dots only
Diagnostics pane shows 4-cell strip + existing export button
Storage pane has Save-to row with Choose… and Reveal, plus preserved delete-all-audio safety alert
All controls bind to existing SettingsStore
Window excluded from screen capture
